title: meriania rugosa
intro: Meriania rugosa, also known as the "bitter tree" or "cajú-bacaba", is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of Meriania rugosa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system and aid in the treatment of certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Meriania rugosa can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
